title: LDAP Authentication Bind Failure After Directory Server Patch
symptoms: LDAP bind operations returning 'Strong auth required' error (LDAP error 8)
Applications using simple bind all failing authentication
Active Directory event log showing LDAP channel binding enforcement
Windows security patch KB5032190 installed at 04:00 UTC
root_cause: Security patch changed LDAP channel binding policy from 'when supported' to 'always required', breaking all legacy applications using simple LDAP bind without channel binding support
resolution: Temporarily reverted LDAP channel binding policy to 'when supported', scheduled application updates to support channel binding, and created migration plan for channel binding enforcement
preventive_actions: Inventory all applications using LDAP simple bind for migration planning
Implement LDAP channel binding support in all application LDAP clients
Create AD patch testing environment mirroring production LDAP usage
Add pre-patch validation of LDAP binding compatibility
Schedule phased LDAP channel binding enforcement with application readiness gates

timeline.event: Security patch KB5032190 installed on ad-dc-london-02
EMEA business hours begin, LDAP bind failures start occurring
IT helpdesk flooded with login failure tickets
Aisha Khan investigates, links failures to overnight patch
LDAP channel binding policy change identified
Channel binding policy reverted to 'when supported'

embedding_summary: LDAP authentication failure after Active Directory security patch enforced channel binding, breaking all applications using simple LDAP bind. 3,100 EMEA employees unable to authenticate for 40 minutes. Resolved by reverting channel binding policy and planning phased migration.
